title: Irrigation Cost Check
description: Industrial-grade tool to evaluate irrigation system cost efficiency, hidden losses, and actionable improvements based on ISO 14046, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Annual water volume used
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of water withdrawn for irrigation per year.

inputs.label: Water cost per m³
inputs.businessContext: Unit cost of water including extraction, treatment, and delivery.

inputs.label: Energy cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Average electricity cost for pumping and distribution.

inputs.label: Pump system efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Overall efficiency of pump and motor system (ISO 9906).

inputs.label: Distribution losses
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of water lost due to leaks, evaporation, and runoff.

inputs.label: Annual labor hours for irrigation
inputs.businessContext: Total labor hours dedicated to irrigation operation and maintenance.

inputs.label: Annual maintenance cost
inputs.businessContext: Total annual cost for repairs, parts, and servicing.

inputs.label: Crop value per hectare
inputs.businessContext: Average revenue generated per hectare from irrigated crops.

inputs.label: Irrigation system type
inputs.businessContext: Type of irrigation system affecting efficiency and cost structure.

inputs.label: Water source
inputs.businessContext: Source of water influencing cost and sustainability.

inputs.label: Automation system installed
inputs.businessContext: Indicates presence of automated scheduling or sensor-based control.

rules.message: Pump efficiency must be between 10% and 100%.
Distribution losses must be between 0% and 50%.
Water volume must be greater than zero.
Irrigated area must be greater than zero.
Drip systems are strongly recommended to have automation for optimal efficiency.

thresholds.message: Water cost exceeds $1.00/m³ – consider water conservation measures.
Pump efficiency below 60% – immediate maintenance or replacement recommended.
Distribution losses above 20% – investigate leaks and improve system.
Labor hours exceed 1000/year – consider automation to reduce costs.

formulas.label: Effective water volume
formulas.description: Volume of water actually reaching crops after distribution losses.

formulas.label: Total water cost
formulas.description: Annual cost of water purchased or extracted.

formulas.label: Total energy cost
formulas.description: Annual energy cost for pumping, assuming 1 kWh per m³ per 100m head (simplified).

formulas.label: Total labor cost
formulas.description: Annual labor cost for irrigation operations.

formulas.label: Total operating cost
formulas.description: Sum of water, energy, labor, and maintenance costs.

formulas.label: Cost per effective m³
formulas.description: Unit cost of water that actually reaches the crop.

formulas.label: Irrigation cost ratio
formulas.description: Ratio of total irrigation cost to crop revenue – primary KPI (Lean Six Sigma).

primary.label: Irrigation cost ratio
primary.description: Primary KPI: fraction of crop revenue consumed by irrigation costs. Target < 0.15 (WERC benchmark).

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Distribution losses impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional cost due to water lost before reaching crops.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Pump inefficiency impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra energy cost due to pump efficiency below 100%.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Labor inefficiency impact
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Estimated excess labor cost if automation is absent (30% waste).

suggestedActions.description: Implement leak detection and repair program to reduce distribution losses by 5%.
Replace pump with high-efficiency model (target 85% efficiency).
Install soil moisture sensors and automated scheduling to reduce water use by 15% and labor by 40%.
Convert from sprinkler to drip irrigation to improve application efficiency by 20%.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Confidence-adjusted irrigation cost ratio
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Primary result adjusted for data uncertainty using a confidence factor (0.8 to 1.2).
